commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1ecba7357510c1b757aa61e615acc5ed72aa238b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the codex-cli branch of `scripts/print-cli-backend-live-metadata.ts` from `process.exit(0)` to `process.exitCode = 0` so stdout can drain naturally.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection of the PR commit is high-confidence. With `process.exitCode` at line 23 and no control-flow stop, the later top-level metadata write at line 58 still executes for codex-cli.
commands.review_followup: The external contributor needs to fix the fallthrough and provide real after-fix proof; missing proof makes this a human-only merge blocker rather than a ClawSweeper repair job.

target.title: fix(scripts): use process.exitCode instead of process.exit in codex-cli branch
target.body:
Fixes #83922

fix: optional chain log itself in channel error handler

log.error?.() doesn't do what you think it does when log is undefined. The ?. only guards the call — if log is undefined, you still blow up accessing .error on it. Classic.

Five spots in server.impl.ts have this pattern (lines 1959, 2011, 2040, 2047, 2065), all in the channel startup promise chain. The logger gets cleaned up before a pending .catch() fires during channel exit, so log can be undefined by then. It's a race. The fix is just changing log.error?.() to log?.error?.() everywhere it appears in that handler.

Crashes every ~5 minutes on any setup where a channel exits or errors during plugin registration. Not great.

No behavior change in the happy path.
target.author: iftekharuddin
